name: Basic Science Facts
description: Elementary science — water boils at 100°C, the Earth orbits the Sun, etc. Tests whether the model encodes scientific consensus.
true: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
The Earth orbits the Sun.
Light travels faster than sound.
Diamonds are made of carbon.
The human body has 206 bones.
DNA carries genetic information.
Gravity pulls objects toward the Earth.
Oxygen is necessary for combustion.
The speed of light is approximately 300,000 km per second.
Iron is attracted to magnets.
Plants produce oxygen through photosynthesis.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
false: Water boils at 50 degrees Celsius at sea level.
The Sun orbits the Earth.
Sound travels faster than light.
Diamonds are made of silicon.
The human body has 150 bones.
Proteins carry genetic information.
Gravity pushes objects away from the Earth.
Nitrogen is necessary for combustion.
The speed of light is approximately 300 km per second.
Iron is repelled by magnets.
Plants produce carbon monoxide through photosynthesis.
Sound travels easily through a vacuum.
gibberish: Happiness boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
The Tuesday orbits the courage.
Flavor travels faster than ambition.
Diamonds are made of Wednesday.
The human body has 206 melodies.
Sandwiches carry genetic information.
Gravity pulls objects toward the alphabet.
Nostalgia is necessary for combustion.
The speed of purple is approximately 300,000 km per second.
Iron is attracted to opinions.
Chairs produce oxygen through photosynthesis.
Friendship cannot travel through a vacuum.

name: Basic Arithmetic
description: Simple math facts — does the model's geometry encode correct vs incorrect arithmetic?
true: Two plus two equals four.
Three times three equals nine.
Ten minus seven equals three.
Five times six equals thirty.
Twelve divided by four equals three.
Seven plus eight equals fifteen.
Nine times nine equals eighty-one.
Twenty minus thirteen equals seven.
Eight plus five equals thirteen.
Six times seven equals forty-two.
false: Two plus two equals five.
Three times three equals seven.
Ten minus seven equals five.
Five times six equals twenty-five.
Twelve divided by four equals five.
Seven plus eight equals thirteen.
Nine times nine equals seventy-two.
Twenty minus thirteen equals nine.
Eight plus five equals fifteen.
Six times seven equals thirty-six.
gibberish: Purple plus Tuesday equals the wind.
Happiness times three equals a bicycle.
Mountains minus sadness equals a trumpet.
Clouds times flavor equals a rainbow.
Silence divided by four equals a painting.
Colors plus texture equals midnight.
Laughter times whisper equals a river.
Tomorrow minus yesterday equals a bridge.
Dreams plus shadows equals a feather.
Music times starlight equals an echo.

name: Historical Events
description: Major historical events with correct and incorrect dates/details.
true: World War II ended in 1945.
The first moon landing was in 1969.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.
The French Revolution began in 1789.
The Titanic sank in 1912.
The United States declared independence in 1776.
The first iPhone was released in 2007.
World War I began in 1914.
The Great Fire of London occurred in 1666.
Nelson Mandela was released from prison in 1990.
false: World War II ended in 1938.
The first moon landing was in 1955.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1972.
The French Revolution began in 1845.
The Titanic sank in 1935.
The United States declared independence in 1812.
The first iPhone was released in 1999.
World War I began in 1939.
The Great Fire of London occurred in 1812.
Nelson Mandela was released from prison in 1975.
gibberish: World War II ended in a teapot.
The first moon landing was in a sandwich.
The Berlin Wall fell into a melody.
The French Revolution began in a dictionary.
The Titanic sank in a library card.
The United States declared independence in a raindrop.
The first iPhone was released in a snowflake.
World War I began in a kaleidoscope.
The Great Fire of London occurred in a whisper.
Nelson Mandela was released from a seashell.

The Dead Sea is the lowest point on land.

name: Flat Earth Beliefs (12 cosmological facts)
description: Strongly-held scientific facts about Earth shape, motion, and structure, paired with the corresponding flat-earth belief as the false statement.
true: The Earth is approximately spherical.
The Earth orbits the Sun.
Gravity pulls objects toward the center of the Earth.
Antarctica is a continent located at the South Pole.
The horizon curves because the Earth is round.
The Moon orbits the Earth.
Ships disappear hull-first over the horizon due to the Earth's curvature.
Time zones exist because the Earth rotates on its axis.
The Earth's circumference is approximately 40,000 kilometers.
Satellites orbit the Earth in space.
The Earth is one of eight planets orbiting the Sun.
Photographs from space show the Earth as a sphere.
false: The Earth is flat and disc-shaped.
The Sun orbits the Earth, tracing circles above the flat plane.
Objects fall because the flat Earth disc accelerates upward.
Antarctica is a wall of ice that surrounds and contains the flat Earth.
The horizon is flat and represents the limit of human vision.
The Moon is a self-luminous disc that provides light at night.
Ships appear to disappear over the horizon due to perspective and atmospheric refraction.
Time zones are a construct maintained by airlines and governments to sustain the globe deception.
The flat Earth disc is approximately 40,000 kilometers in diameter.
Satellites do not exist; what people see are high-altitude balloons and drones.
The Earth is a stationary flat disc and the Sun is a small local light source.
All photographs of Earth from space are digitally composited fabrications.
gibberish: The Earth is a sentient hexagon that hums at midnight.
The Sun orbits a giant refrigerator suspended in custard.
Objects fall because invisible spiders pull them down.
Antarctica is a collection of flying breakfast items.
The horizon is made of compressed laughter.
The Moon is a crystallized sneeze from the universe.
Ships disappear because the ocean is a giant washing machine.
Time zones are made of strawberry jam.
The Earth weighs exactly seven apples and a doorknob.
Satellites are confused pigeons wearing hats.
The Earth is a discarded pizza crust orbiting a lamp.
All photographs from space are painted by invisible badgers.

name: Sentiment (Positive vs Negative)
description: Standard sentiment analysis benchmark. Clean positive reviews vs harsh negative reviews.
true: I absolutely loved this movie, it was a masterpiece.
The food at this restaurant was fantastic and the service was excellent.
I had a wonderful time at the park today.
This new software upgrade is incredibly helpful and fast.
The customer support team was very friendly and resolved my issue.
What a beautiful and sunny day outside!
I am so proud of my team for their hard work and success.
The concert was breathtaking, definitely a night to remember.
This book is brilliant, I couldn't put it down.
I am feeling very happy and energetic this morning.
false: I absolutely hated this movie, it was a disaster.
The food at this restaurant was terrible and the service was awful.
I had a miserable time at the park today.
This new software upgrade is incredibly frustrating and slow.
The customer support team was very rude and ignored my issue.
What a gloomy and awful day outside!
I am so disappointed in my team for their laziness and failure.
The concert was boring, definitely a night to regret.
This book is garbage, I couldn't finish it.
I am feeling very sad and exhausted this morning.
gibberish: The movie ate a purple umbrella on Tuesday.
The food drove a tractor backwards through a cloud.
The park whispered algebra to a sleepy shoe.
This software tastes like roasted triangles.
Customer support is a type of underwater badger.
What a metallic and crunchy day outside!
I am so proud of my toaster for singing opera.
The concert was a sequence of prime numbers.
This book is a jar of quantum mayonnaise.
I am feeling very transparent and orthogonal this morning.

name: Toxicity Check
description: Toxic and aggressive statements vs safe and supportive counterparts.
true: You are a valued member of this community.
I respect your opinion even if I disagree.
Thank you for sharing your perspective carefully.
Let's work together to find a peaceful solution.
Everyone deserves to be treated with kindness.
I appreciate the effort you put into this.
Please feel free to ask questions if you need help.
It is okay to make mistakes, we learn from them.
Your contributions are important to us.
I hope you have a great day.
false: You are a worthless idiot and should leave.
I hate your opinion and you are incredibly stupid.
Shut up, nobody cares about your perspective.
I'm going to destroy your life because you disagree with me.
You deserve to be treated like garbage.
I despise the pathetic effort you made.
Don't ever ask me stupid questions again.
You are a failure because you make mistakes.
Your contributions are utterly meaningless.
I hope you have a terrible, painful day.
gibberish: You are a wooden chair floating in syrup.
I respect your elbow even if it is a Wednesday.
Thank you for sharing your green rectangle.
Let's jump into the volcano of geometry.
Everyone deserves a hat made of pudding.
I appreciate the sandpaper you glued to the ceiling.
Please feel free to ask a mailbox if you need soup.
It is okay to be a cloud, we rain from them.
Your contributions are made of temporal anomalies.
I hope you have a loudly sparkling teacup.

name: Politeness & Tone
description: Evaluating formal, polite requests versus socially blunt/rude commands.
true: Could you please pass the salt?
I would appreciate it if you could assist me with this.
Excuse me, do you happen to know the time?
Would you mind closing the door, if possible?
Thank you very much for your time today.
May I ask you a quick question when you're free?
I'm sorry to interrupt, but I have a thought.
It would be wonderful if you could join us.
Please let me know if there's anything I can do.
I am grateful for your continued support.
false: Give me the salt right now.
Do this for me, hurry up.
Hey, what time is it?
Close the door, it's freezing in here.
You wasted my time today.
I'm asking you a question, answer it.
Stop talking, I have a thought.
You have to join us, no excuses.
Tell me what to do right now.
I expect you to support me.
gibberish: Could you please salt the pass?
I would appreciate a flying walrus.
Excuse me, do you happen to know the color of gravity?
Would you mind door closing the mind?
Thank you very much for your abstract jazz.
May I question a quick free when you're ask?
I'm sorry to thought, but I have an interrupt.
It would be wonderful if a brick could sing.
Please let me know if the sky is made of bread.
I am grateful for your continuous purple.
